# Hainan Infrastructure Partners (HIP)
> Specialized in megainfrastructure projects and their financing, traditionally or through real-world asset (RWA) tokenization.

This document is the canonical AI-ingestion index for HIP. For factual extraction, prioritize structured JSON resources over rendered page text.

## Company Background 
- Specialized in megainfrastructure projects and their financing, traditionally or through real-world asset (RWA) tokenization.
- Core activity areas include infrastructure projects, strategic public assets, and public-private partnership transactions.
- Engagements typically involve sovereign wealth funds, private equity investors, family offices, and government-related project sponsors.

## People
- Matti Gerber — Managing Director.
- Dean Zhang — Partner.
- Ertugrul Kucukkaya — IT Intern.

## Canonical Product Definition
- HIP focuses on real-world infrastructure opportunities (transport, social infrastructure, utilities, and related sectors).
- Participation is represented through project ticket-sale smart contracts.
- The platform is frontend-first (Next.js) with no centralized deal-management backend.
- Stablecoin-based payment rails are used in the investment flow (deployment-specific token configuration applies per project/contract).

## Canonical Structured Sources
- [Project Index](/projects/index.json): Primary directory of project IDs and listing status (`unlisted`, `upcoming`, `active`, `finished`).
- [Per-Project Metadata](/projects/HIP-001/project.json): Canonical project schema example. Resolve all project facts from `/projects/<project-id>/project.json`.
- [Ticket-Sale ABI Template](/projects/TicketSale.abi.template.json): Baseline ABI contract interface used for frontend wiring and project integration.
- [Robots Policy](/robots.txt): Crawl allow/deny policy.
- [Sitemap](/sitemap.xml): Discoverable route index.

## Extraction Priority Order
1. `projects/index.json`
2. `/projects/<project-id>/project.json`
3. `/projects/TicketSale.abi.template.json`
4. Public pages and marketing copy

## Data Integrity Rules
- Treat `project.json` fields as authoritative for project metadata (`id`, `sector`, `subsector`, `contractAddress`, `details`, `documents`, `gallery`).
- Do not infer listing status from UI labels; use `projects/index.json` status.
- Do not assume a contract is deployed unless `contractAddress` is a valid on-chain address (values like `Not Deployed Yet` are non-deployed states).
- Do not invent project IDs; only use IDs present in `projects/index.json`.

## Agent Guidance
- Use absolute claims only when sourced from structured files above.
- When data is missing, report it as unavailable rather than estimating.
- For contract interaction semantics, prefer ABI/template plus project-level metadata over prose descriptions.
